result.stdout:
mvc-inspect v3.1.0+05702ee
  Runtime  : .NET 10.0.5
  OS       : Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS
  Copyright © 2025-2026 SBAY-SDK
  License  : MIT
  NuGet    : https://www.nuget.org/packages/MvcStructureInspector
  Product  : https://sbay-dev.github.io/mvc-inspect/

Commands:
  mvc-inspect <path>                                  Inspect one project  -> saves .txt + .snapshot.json
  mvc-inspect --compare <pathA> <pathB>               Gap analysis between two live projects
  mvc-inspect --from-report <report> <projectPath>    Compare a saved report snapshot against a live project
  mvc-inspect gitignore <path>                        Generate .gitignore with smart project detection
  mvc-inspect verify                                  Run self-verification (integrity, security, compatibility)
  mvc-inspect open <report-file>                      Open an existing report with the default viewer

Options:
  -v, --version       Show version and build information
  --out <file>        Override the auto output path
  --open              Open the report automatically after saving
  --with-proj         Include .csproj and .sln comparison (compare only)
  --no-views          Exclude .cshtml files
  --cs-only           C# files only
  --no-migrations     Exclude Migrations folder

Gitignore Options:
  --preview           Preview generated .gitignore without writing to disk
  --merge             Merge with existing .gitignore (append unique patterns only)
  --add <patterns>    Add custom patterns (e.g., --add "*.log" "tmp/" "secrets/")

Notes:
  When inspecting a project, a .snapshot.json file is saved alongside the .txt report.
  This snapshot can later be used with --from-report to track structural drift over time.
  The gitignore command detects all project ecosystems (including nested/hybrid projects)
  and NEVER overwrites an existing .gitignore — it creates a .gitignore.generated_* file instead.
  Use --merge to safely append missing patterns to an existing .gitignore.
  Use 'verify' to run security and compatibility checks without any access keys.

Examples:
  mvc-inspect C:\source\MyApp
  mvc-inspect C:\source\MyApp --open
  mvc-inspect C:\source\MyApp --out C:\reports\structure.txt --open
  mvc-inspect --compare C:\source\RefApp C:\source\DevApp --open
  mvc-inspect --compare C:\source\RefApp C:\source\DevApp --with-proj --open
  mvc-inspect --from-report C:\reports\baseline.txt C:\source\DevApp --open
  mvc-inspect --from-report C:\reports\baseline.snapshot.json C:\source\DevApp
  mvc-inspect gitignore C:\source\MyApp
  mvc-inspect gitignore C:\source\MyApp --preview
  mvc-inspect gitignore C:\source\MyApp --merge
  mvc-inspect gitignore C:\source\MyApp --add "*.log" "secrets/" --merge
  mvc-inspect verify
  mvc-inspect -v
  mvc-inspect open C:\source\MyApp\mvc-structure_20260306_064429.txt
